classification.rationale: This thread documents an evolving sales process with multiple stakeholders, stage progression from eval to commercials, and active pricing negotiation — not a contract document with redlines, so DealView is appropriate over ContractView. The security questionnaire attachment is a process artifact, not a contract with clause-level redlines, so prefer_document_over_thread_when_redlines_present does not trigger. Open blockers with owners (Raj on pricing, Erin on infosec), a sentiment drop flagged by Ben in s12, and the counterparty's last pricing ask all need to be surfaced per extract_blockers_with_owners, flag_sentiment_drops, and surface_counterparty_last_ask.

state.stage_rationale: Two parallel blockers — Raj has not responded to Alex's pricing structures offered on 2026-04-11, and Erin's security review queue is 2–3 weeks with an incomplete package — have halted forward progress as of Ben's transparency note on 2026-04-22.

top_actions.title: Get Raj's response on pricing counter-structures
top_actions.detail: Raj rejected $180k on 2026-04-10 but has not replied to the usage-based or phased options presented on 2026-04-11; deal is blocked on his decision.

top_actions.title: Deliver complete security package to Erin
top_actions.detail: SOC 2 Type II, subprocessor list, pen test summary, completed questionnaire, and EU/UK residency controls must be submitted before Erin's 2–3 week review clock starts.

top_actions.title: Book 30-min pricing call with Raj and Lisa
top_actions.detail: Alex proposed this call on 2026-04-11 to walk through both pricing structures but no meeting has been confirmed.

top_actions.title: Confirm eval completion and sign-off
top_actions.detail: The 3-week eval started ~2026-03-22; results were positive at week 1 but no formal close-out or success criteria sign-off has been recorded.

top_actions.title: Align on revised close timeline given delays
top_actions.detail: Ben flagged on 2026-04-22 that Q2 close is at risk; a revised target date needs to be agreed with Lisa and Raj to preserve forecast integrity.

open_blockers.description: Raj has not responded to the usage-based (~$120k floor) or phased ($130k Year 1) pricing structures offered on 2026-04-11; original $180k ACV was rejected as roughly double their benchmarked band of $90–110k.

Security review requires a complete package (SOC 2 Type II, subprocessors, pen test, questionnaire, EU/UK data residency) before Erin will start her 2–3 week review; package has not yet been submitted.
Ben flagged that security backlog from Q1 audit is adding further delay beyond the standard queue, putting the stated Q2 decision timeline at risk.

next_step.description: Alex to submit the complete security package to Erin and simultaneously push Ben to lock in a 30-minute pricing call with Raj and Lisa this week to unblock the commercial conversation.

key_moments.summary: Discovery call yielded clear, prioritized decision criteria (lineage > ops burden > cost) and a Q2 close commitment — deal entered structured eval with high mutual alignment.
Ben reported lineage replay worked out of the box on the order-events stream — the core pain point was resolved in week 1, building strong champion conviction.
Lisa Park (VP) proactively entered the thread and signaled readiness to run commercials in parallel with eval, indicating executive sponsorship.
CFO Raj Mehta entered and rejected the $180k ACV as nearly double the benchmarked band, introducing a hard price anchor and a new decision-maker not previously in the deal.
Erin Schulz (infosec) introduced a formal security review process with a 2–3 week queue, adding a previously unknown gate before any commitment can be made.
Ben proactively disclosed that both pricing and security are unresolved and that Q2 timing is in jeopardy — deal is stalled on two simultaneous blockers.
